The '''Miy'til starfighter''' was a fighter unique to the [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium]], where it served as the primary superiority fighter for the [[Hapan Royal Navy]].

==Characteristics==
[[File:Miytil-SotGSE.png|thumb|left|180px|A Miy'til starfighter.]]

The craft's small size meant that the Hapan engineers had to miniaturize many of its components. It employed an [[Astromech droid/Legends|Astromech droid]] to assist with in-flight operations and navigation. The Miy'til was, like other small starfighters, relatively lightly armed, with only two [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] and one [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missile]] launcher. A command version of the starfighter was more expensive, but equipped with another [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]], two [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]], and another missile launcher.<ref name="SOTG07" />

Each [[Nova-class battle cruiser|''Nova''-class battle cruiser]] carried 24 Miy'til starfighters, while 36 were carried aboard a [[Hapan Battle Dragon]]. The [[Queen Mother]]'s mobile palace ''[[Star Home]]'' carried 60 of the fighters.

==History==
[[File:Miytil_schem.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Miy'til [[:Category:Technical drawing images|schematics]].]]

Following their defeat in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], several [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] starfighter engineers fled into the [[Hapes Cluster/Legends|Hapes Cluster]], seeking refuge. There, they lent their services to the Consortium, helping to develop new fighter designs.<ref name="SOTG07" />

The Miy'til fighters saw service in many of the border skirmishes the Hapans had with pirates or other interlopers, the [[Battle of Dathomir (Hunt for Zsinj)|Battle of Dathomir]], the [[Battle of Fondor]], and naturally, during the [[Battle of Hapes (Yuuzhan Vong War)|Battle of Hapes]] against the [[Yuuzhan Vong]]. Interestingly, they served alongside [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]] in the Hapan fleet.

[[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Isolder]] had a modified version of this fighter, the [[Storm (Miy'til starfighter)|''Storm'']], which included a turbo booster engine similar in design to the [[SubLight Acceleration Motor/Legends|SLAM]] system that was used on [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Missile Boat]]s, allowing it to briefly attain speeds roughly 33% faster than the notably fast [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|RZ-1 A-wing interceptor]]. The ''Storm'' was also much more heavily armed, mounting three laser cannons, two [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]], a concussion missile launcher, and two [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]] bombing tubes.<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels">''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''</ref>

These fighters continued to be in use as of the [[Heritage Council|Hapan Insurrection]], participating on both sides of the engagement. A squadron of the fighters loyal to the [[Galney (Ducha of Terephon)|Ducha Galney]] strafed [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] and [[Zekk]] when they investigated her estate, and many of them were present in the [[Battle of Hapes (Second Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Hapes]] in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]].
